questions.q: What is the correct representation of the electronic configuration of hydrogen?
Which of the following is a characteristic feature of hydrogen?
options: a. It is a noble gas
b. It is a metalloidal element
c. It is an active metal

questions.q: Why does hydrogen show only one oxidation state in its compounds?
questions.answer: Due to the small size of hydrogen and the presence of only one electron in the outermost shell, it shows only one oxidation state (+1) in its compounds.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Hydrogen is a very light gas and its density is very __________ compared to air.
What is the correct statement regarding the properties of hydrogen?
Write the electronic configuration of hydrogen atom in terms of principal, azimuthal and magnetic quantum numbers.
questions.answer: The electronic configuration of hydrogen atom is 1s1. The principal quantum number (n) is 1, the azimuthal quantum number (l) is 0, and the magnetic quantum number (m) is 0.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a correct representation of the electronic configuration of hydrogen?
What are the two most common methods of preparing hydrogen?
questions.answer: Electrolysis of water and cracking of petroleum.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Hydrogen is a highly __________ gas and it is highly flammable.
What is the correct statement regarding the physical properties of hydrogen?
questions.statement: Hydrogen is a solid at room temperature.
